modules.description: The 6000-level course gives the student an understanding of aircraft rigid body motion. Students will learn how to compare various aircraft and predict their dynamics based on an understanding of the aircraft equations of motion and analysis techniques.

tlos: TLO 1. Describe the fundamental principles governing the rigid-body equations of motion for aircraft.
TLO 2. Explain the differences between body-axis, stability-axis, and wind-axis reference frames in aircraft dynamics.
TLO 3. Apply the six-degree-of-freedom equations of motion to determine aircraft translational and rotational motion.
TLO 4. Characterize the effects of simplifying assumptions on the nonlinear and linearized equations of motion.
TLO 5. Compute aircraft dynamic responses using numerical methods to solve the equations of motion.
TLO 6. Discuss modern methods of interpreting equations of motion, such as uncertainty quantification and sensitivity analysis.

events.description: The rigid body equations of motion for an aircraft are derived, and then trim solutions are presented. The equations are linearized in both state space as well as transfer function methods.

tlos: TLO 1. Explain concepts of aircraft static and dynamic stability
TLO 2. Collect data to determine aircraft stability characteristics
TLO 3. Analyze data from flight test to determine aircraft stability characteristics
TLO 4. Compare flight test data to Mil-Std-1797B.

events.description: The concepts of static and dynamic stability are applied to aircraft. Aircraft modes of motion are introduced. Stability derivatives and their influence are presented. Finally, MIL-STD 1797B guidelines are introduced and applied to the static and dynamic stability parameters to predict an aircraft's handling qualities or to assess a specification compliance.

This event introduces several Flight Test Techniques (FTTs) used in flying qualities testing and then uses those FTTs to explore how changing stability derivatives of an aircraft changes its flying qualities.
These sorties explore the s-plane to further explore how FTTs are used to investigate an aircraft, and to identify and qualify modes of motion.
This event introduces open loop flying qualities of an unpowered aircraft.
This event introduces the students to FTTs used to assess an aircraft's static stability and to assess against a military specification.
This event introduces students to FTTs used to assess a fighter type aircraft's dynamic stability flying qualities and shows the dependence of flying qualities on flight condition.

tlos: TLO 1. Evaluate an aircraft's unaugmented static stability flying qualities against those found in MIL-STD 1797B. The student will also be able to identify and use unaugmented dynamic stability criteria found in MIL-STD 1797B.

events.eventName: T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Brief
events.description: This event introduces the students to the flying qualities T-38 test plan.

events.description: These are student designed and planned flights allowing the students to gather data in support of their flying qualities test plan report.

modules.description: The 7000-level course provides the student with an understanding of aircraft linear control systems as applied to aircraft. Students will learn basic linear analysis techniques and how to evaluate a control system's performance as related to piloted and unpiloted air vehicles.

tlos: TLO 1. Explain classical and modern flight control systems' structure and expected behavior.
TLO 2. Demonstrate how to use Bode and root locus tools to evaluate the dynamics of a feedback control system.
TLO 3. Examine control filters and compensators and their impact to the control systems response.
TLO 4. Demonstrate usage of MIL-STD-1797 to predict flying and handling qualities.
TLO 5. Interpret the characteristics of pilot-induced oscillations.

events.description: Feedback flight control systems are introduced along with motivation for transition from mechanical to automatic flight control systems.

tlos: TLO 1. Describe basic aircraft structural components, frame of reference, structural principles related to airframe loads and flutter analysis.
TLO 2. Explain the structures discipline in terms of subdisciplines including Airframe Loads, Aeroelasticity, and Aerodynamic and Inertial Loads with Aeroelastic Response.
TLO 3. Develop structures test methodology, test series unique considerations, and instrumentation requirements for given aircraft structural modifications.
TLO 4. Execute elevated risk structures flight test from the cockpit or the control room using buildup principles.

events.description: Static structural analysis is presented along with basic stress/strain analysis.

events.eventName: Flight Control Systems Development and Integration Testing Lecture
events.description: The testing process used to clear flight control algorithms for flight test is covered.

events.eventName: Learjet Flight Control Systems Verification Ground Test MIB
events.description: This event explores how flight control system verification and validation is executed.

prerequisites.name: Flight Control Systems Development and Integration Testing Lecture

events.eventName: Learjet Flight Control Systems Verification Ground Test

prerequisites.name: Flight Control Systems Development and Integration Testing Lecture

events.description: This flight is the students' first look at how a structural envelope expansion test might be constructed and executed.

events.eventName: F-16 Structures Envelope Expansion Control Room

events.description: This flight is the students' first look at how a Flutter or LCO test might be constructed and executed.

tlos: TLO 9. Understand the use of different control methods and their applications.
TLO 10. Understand the use of different simulations and their applications.
TLO 11. Understand different data collection methods and their applications.
TLO 12. Evaluate the sim to real gap for a given scenario.
TLO 13. Create hazard and risk mitigation strategies for various autonomy scenarios.
TLO 14. Apply control methods to different applications.
TLO 15. Analyze robustness methods and training tools for their accuracy and proper information.
TLO 16. Analyze methods for testing autonomous systems.

events.description: This block will cover advanced Data-Driven control techniques.

modules.description: The 8000-level course takes all the material introduced previously in the flying qualities curriculum and applies it to handling qualities evaluation.

tlos: TLO 1. Understand the basic vocabulary of handling qualities testing.
TLO 2. Evaluate an aircraft using the Cooper-Harper rating scale.
TLO 3. Evaluate an aircraft for PIO tendencies.
TLO 4. Understand the differences of handling qualities of piloted and unpiloted aircraft.
TLO 5. Evaluate the dynamics of an aircraft based on MIL-STD-1797B.

events.description: The Cooper Harper rating scale is introduced. PIO rating scales are also introduced.

prerequisites.name: Flight Control Systems Development and Integration Testing Lecture
Flight Control Systems Development and Integration Testing Lecture

modules.description: The 9000-level course demonstrates how the buildup approach can be used for handling qualities testing in different phases of vehicle flight testing.

tlos: TLO 1. Recall tenants of failure modes testing.

events.description: Various vehicle failure modes are evaluated in this block.

tlos: TLO 1. Describe the mechanics and contributors to aircraft stalls.
TLO 2. Describe the mechanics and dangers of inertial and kinematic coupling.
TLO 3. Describe the mechanics of spins and the aircraft properties that contribute to them.
TLO 4. Determine how to recover from spins in various aircraft types.
TLO 5. Explain how to conduct stall, departure, and spin testing.

tlos: TLO 1. Recall the process of certifying aircraft stores.

events.description: The analytical process and flight test methods used to qualify stores on aircraft for safe operation are introduced.

tlos: TLO 1. Describe how the concept of build-up approach is applied to first flights and envelope expansion testing.
TLO 2. Describe how risk mitigation is addressed in test planning and mission materials in a first flight and in envelope expansion.
TLO 3. Describe the types of testing that are done during ground testing, low-speed taxi, and high-speed taxi.
TLO 4. Describe how the elements comprising a maneuver block are determined.
TLO 5. Given an FTT, describe what information is provided and which test objectives can be met.
TLO 6. Describe the general flight conditions and configurations that should be tested during a first flight.
TLO 7. List the major components of a generic first flight profile.
